references.passiveText: There's a garden on the border between two feuding properties. Somehow, both families tend it without ever meeting.
references.triggerDialogue: One family plants in the morning, the other waters in the evening. Neither acknowledges the other's work, but the garden thrives. {target_npc} says it started when a child from one family dropped seeds on the property line and a child from the other watered them out of curiosity. Years later, it's the most beautiful patch of green in {location}. Proof that even in the middle of a feud, growing things is easier than growing grudges.

references.passiveText: In some settlements, travelers are welcomed with open arms. In others, they're watched until they leave. The difference is always one person's influence.
references.triggerDialogue: {target_npc} runs a small inn near {location} and makes a point of treating every stranger like a returning friend. Warm food, a clean bed, and conversation without judgment. They say the settlement changed after they opened: fewer suspicions, more curiosity about the outside world. One person's hospitality shifted an entire community's attitude. It doesn't always work that way, but when it does, it's something to see.
